_comment: Parakeet TDT per-tensor tolerances for compare_tensors.py.
Reference: parakeet-mlx (Apple Silicon) running fp32 inference.
C++: ggml CPU fp32 compute, weights stored as fp32 in GGUF.
The mel frontend runs STFT in fp64 (C++) vs fp32 (MLX/ONNX), so
mel.in can have ~5e-4 max_abs in the top mel bins. This is well
within the ONNX golden test tolerance (5e-4 max, 5e-5 mean) set
in frontend_smoke.cpp. The error attenuates through the 24-layer
conformer: block 0 max_abs ~1e-4, block 23 ~1e-4.
Encoder final output (enc.final) uses the values validated by
encoder_smoke.cpp: max_abs 1e-4, mean_abs 1e-6 on CPU. Metal
uses wider tolerances (max_abs 1e-2, mean_abs 1e-3) due to
reduced-precision matmul kernels.
Decoder dumps (dec.*) are host-side LSTM/joint intermediates,
matched via dump_reference.py decode subcommand. The LSTM states
(dec.lstm.<layer>.{h,c}.0) take zero input + zero state at step 0,
so they are independent of encoder output and should match to
within fp32 round-off. The joint output (dec.joint.0) depends on
the encoder, so its tolerance covers mel + encoder drift.
Note: these tolerances are currently used by compare_tensors.py
and dump_reference.py workflows. The C++ smoke tests
(frontend_smoke.cpp, encoder_smoke.cpp) still hardcode their own
constants. Wiring the C++ tests to read this file is tracked as a
follow-up.
